reasoning: The downgrade sentinel is set in ssl_fill_hello_random (ssl/s3_lib.c:5253-5288), called from tls_early_post_process_client_hello at line 2112 with the dgrd value determined by ssl_choose_server_version. The flow is: (1) dgrd is initialized to DOWNGRADE_NONE at line 1861; (2) ssl_choose_server_version calls check_for_downgrade which sets dgrd = DOWNGRADE_TO_1_2 when negotiating TLS 1.2 with TLS 1.3 supported, or dgrd = DOWNGRADE_TO_1_1 when negotiating below TLS 1.2 with TLS 1.2 supported; (3) ssl_fill_hello_random fills the full 32-byte random with random data via RAND_bytes_ex, then overwrites the last sizeof(tls12downgrade) (8) bytes with tls12downgrade if DOWNGRADE_TO_1_2, or last sizeof(tls11downgrade) (8) bytes with tls11downgrade if DOWNGRADE_TO_1_1. This satisfies both requirements: the last 8 bytes are set to the downgrade sentinel, and the remaining bytes are random. The tls_construct_server_hello function then simply copies s->s3.server_random (already filled with the sentinel) into the ServerHello packet at line 2595.

evidence.expected_behavior: When the server negotiates TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.1, the last 8 bytes of the ServerHello random field must be overwritten with the appropriate downgrade sentinel value (tls12downgrade or tls11downgrade).
